alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required via Codex maximum levels (MLs) or guideline levels. Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed to protect human health, keeping levels as low as possible based on scientific evidence. Refer to the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must comply with food safety criteria as laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005. These criteria define the acceptability of a product or a batch of food applicable to products placed on the market.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Listeria monocytogenes
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Shelf-life studies (durability studies and challenge tests) must comply with food safety criteria defined in Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005. Technical guidance documents provide detailed information on how to conduct shelf-life studies and evaluate laboratory competence for Listeria monocytogenes in RTE foods.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Production process hygiene
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must comply with process hygiene criteria as laid down in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005. These criteria indicate the correct functioning of the production process.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not directly added to kueh; allowed in fillings/toppings within limits. Class II chemical preservatives should not be directly added when manufacturing kueh. They can only be directly added, within prescribed regulatory limits, to fillings and toppings.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Compliance with regulatory limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must comply. All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food additive safety assessment
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. New: Only SFA-permitted additives. Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Unpermitted additive prohibition
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not allowed. If a food product contains an additive that is not permitted, it must not be imported or sold. An application can be submitted to SFA for evaluation.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Compliance with regulatory limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must comply. All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food and also with the Regulatory Limits for Food Additives.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (incidental constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits. New: Within SFA's regulatory limits. Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Exceeding contaminant limits prohibition
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not allowed. If food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, they must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Veterinary drug residues (diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, dienoestrol)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing residues of diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, or dienoestrol is prohibited.

alerts.title: Modified: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Declaration of ingredients and additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: Old: Must be declared on food labels. New: Must be declared. All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products for sale in Singapore must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Labelling Requirement - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Required to be labeled if present as an ingredient or food additive. These are mandatory labeling allergens.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Labelling Requirement - Sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10 or more. Mandatory labeling if the concentration of residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Prohibited Foods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from production, import, or sale. A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale exists. Verification of this list is required.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required via GSFA Online Database. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets conditions for permitted food additives in all foods. The online database provides searchable provisions by additive, functional class, and food category, and is regularly updated.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via GSFA Online Database (Codex STAN 192-1995). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins in Spices
alerts.details: Old: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required against Union list in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008. This database serves as a tool to inform about approved food additives and their conditions of use.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Salmonella prevalence
alerts.details: New: Verification required against specific regulations for special guarantees (e.g., Council Decision 95/410/EC, Decision 2004/235/EC, Decision 2003/644/EC, Regulation (EC) No 1688/2005, Regulation (EC) No 853/2004, Regulation (EC) No 2160/2003). Special guarantees apply to trade from Sweden, Finland, Norway, and other EU countries with control programmes recognized as equivalent.

alerts.details: Old: Must conform to Japan's Specifications and Standards for Food Additives. Verification required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Additive Limits - Ingredients of food or additives, production/processing/use/cooking/preservation methods

alerts.details: Old: Must not exceed quantities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm to human health, unless specific residual limits are established. Verification required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Toxic or harmful substances, foreign substances

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Additive limits verification
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Different limits apply depending on the additive and food product type. Use the 'List of Permitted Food Additives' or 'Food Additives Search tool' and 'Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives' to check.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Multiple Class II chemical preservatives
alerts.details: New: Calculation tool required. If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used, the 'Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives' must be used to calculate permitted levels.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II Chemical Preservatives (multiple)
alerts.details: Old: Calculation tool must be used. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Additive Limits - Food Additive Limits

alerts.details: Old: Must be assessed and permitted by SFA. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Additive Limits - Non-permitted Food Additives
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited from import or sale. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminant limits verification
alerts.details: New: Verification required. If laboratory test results indicate contaminants, they must be within specified limits. Refer to relevant documents for Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, and Other incidental constituents.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Veterinary drug residues (other MRLs)
alerts.details: New: Verification required. SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, effective from 1 May 2020. These MRLs can be downloaded from the SFA website.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants exceeding limits
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited from import or sale. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
